REN-007 Adjusted Analyses Report: All-Cohort Composite-Component Event Subset

1 Population Summary

This report reproduces the adjusted analysis workflow from the project R Markdown analyses, but it restricts the cohort to subjects who had at least one of the component events referenced in the PH-10.1c composite table categories: death, AKI requiring dialysis, or 30-day cardiopulmonary readmission.

1.1 Site-Restricted Event-Positive Subset

Using all available ADSL records, the cohort included 454 randomized subjects overall and 418 evaluable ITT1FL = "Y" subjects. Applying the component-event subset definition retained 37 subjects total: 19 on RBT-1 and 18 on Placebo.

The exact subject listing used for this report is also written to output/allcohort_component_event_subjects.csv, with overlap counts in output/allcohort_component_event_overlap_summary.csv.

1.2 Component Counts by Treatment

Component Event Counts in the All-Cohort Event-Positive Subset
Treatment Subjects Death AKI requiring dialysis 30-day cardiopulmonary readmission At least 2 component events
Placebo 18 7 6 11 6
RBT-1 19 3 6 13 3

1.5 Statistical Analysis

The analysis code follows the same structure as the existing project reports: treatment groups are Placebo and RBT-1, medical-history burden is derived from ADMH, clinical baseline covariates are derived from ADSC, efficacy endpoints are modeled with propensity-adjusted regression, and postoperative laboratory endpoints are aligned to surgery date and analyzed in first-week and long-term windows. Because this subset contains only 37 subjects, the smaller-sample models are allowed to return descriptive non-estimable notes instead of stopping the report knit.

2 Adjusted Efficacy Analyses

2.1 Binary Endpoints

Adjusted Binary Efficacy Results
Logistic regression adjusted using pre-surgery medical history summary
Endpoint Model Odds ratio (RBT-1 vs Placebo) 95%% CI P-value Model note
Death Logistic regression 0.29 0.06 to 1.40 0.124 NA
AKI requiring dialysis Logistic regression 0.91 0.23 to 3.63 0.891 NA
30-day cardiopulmonary readmission Logistic regression 1.37 0.35 to 5.35 0.648 NA
